cycle_label: GOV-05 — governance-thread standalone sprint · clean health-check sweep + GOV-04 F2/F3 route-out verification · runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade

plain_english._doc: Operator-facing summary block. /brief reads this to emit a plain-language header. The governance thread is NOT a cycle — it is a thread of standalone GOV-NN sprints. cycle_number tracks the GOV sprint number (GOV-05 = 5). Re-author this block at each GOV sprint OPEN.
plain_english.summary: The governance thread runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade — not a 4th nephew. It scopes from a health-check sweep, takes ownerless cross-cutting work the cascade structurally can't absorb, and codifies findings to memory + DAcumen. GOV-05 CLOSED 2026-05-14, same-day open-to-close, two loops clean. L01 ran a fresh health-check sweep — it came back CLEAN — and verified GOV-04's F2/F3 route-out LANDED: F2 (telemetry-contract checker now scheduled, fires 23:47 nightly) and F3 (all 5 telemetry contracts passing) both resolved by the telemetry-contract surface owner, neither re-surfaced as ownerless. L02 codified the durable finding — a GOV route-out is a closed loop, the next fresh sweep is the verification gate that confirms the owner acted (memory/route-out-verification-gate.md) — and closed the sprint. No carryover, no escalations, standing instruments green. GOV-06 opens from a fresh sweep when next scheduled.
goals: L01 — fresh health-check sweep + GOV-04 F2/F3 route-out verification. DONE. Sweep clean across all surfaces: 0 failed `systemctl --user` units, casey-pipeline + prod casey-junior healthy, rag indexers (darntech + dellatech) ran clean today, health-refresh-check green (all 6 pipelines ok). F2 route-out verified: observatory-telemetry-contract-check.timer is now enabled + active (fires 23:47 nightly) — the telemetry-contract surface owner scheduled it. F3 route-out verified: telemetry-contract-status.json (2026-05-14 21:33) shows 5 pass / 0 fail / 0 warn — the owner closed the failing contract. Neither re-surfaced as GOV-shaped: the route-out worked.

L02 — codify route-out-verification finding + close. DONE. Codified memory/route-out-verification-gate.md: a GOV route-out is a closed loop, not fire-and-forget — the next GOV fresh sweep is the verification gate that confirms the owner picked the item up. GOV-02/03/04 routed work out with paper trails; GOV-05's sweep is the first to run that paper trail as a closed test and confirm the resolved-by-owner outcome. GOV-05 closed — clean sweep, route-out round-trip proven, nothing to carry.
plain_english.proposed_next_action: GOV-05 is CLOSED — both loops clean, no carryover, no escalations, nothing on fire. This cycle.json sits in the between-sprints state until GOV-06 opens. To open GOV-06: run a fresh health-check sweep FIRST (per the operating model — scope from the sweep, never from a hunch or a carryover list). The sweep covers: systemctl --user services + timers (look for failed/stale units), observatory data freshness, the casey-pipeline trigger path, cross-BU service health (dev casey-pipeline + prod casey-junior + both rag indexers), and the standing instruments — health-refresh-check.timer and observatory-telemetry-contract-check.timer (both now self-reporting; a red checker is a real finding). Then scope GOV-06's queue from what the sweep surfaces, re-author this plain_english block, and flip status → open. Known dormant item (not a carryover, just context): cycle-09 WS-B2 Nextcloud content-sync auto-mirror substrate — deferred in GOV-04 L02 with a context note, cycle-30+ discretionary, not critical; only pull it into a GOV sprint if the sweep shows it's become needful.

cycle_mode_notes: GOV sprints do not pillar-rotate and do not join the cascade. cascade_mode is standalone by design — the three-sprint cascade is capped at 3 (three is the floor AND the ceiling). GOV takes ownerless cross-cutting work, never nephew COLLECT-queue work.
structure: Health-check sweep scopes the queue → GOV executes the ownerless backlog as a standalone sprint → findings codified to memory + DAcumen
cascade_mode: standalone — runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade, not a 4th nephew
authored_by: operator-direct — GOV-05 opened after GOV-04 closed clean. Scoped from a fresh health-check sweep per the operating model (scope from the sweep, never from a carryover list). L01 sweep ran same-session and came back clean — the headline finding is positive: GOV-04's F2/F3 route-out to the telemetry-contract surface owner landed and is verified.
stakes: The governance thread is cross-BU and standalone — nesting its sprint-logs inside one BU's foreman (darntech) stranded them when the active cycle moved, and made /brief unrunnable from the governance workspace. Its own foreman-enabled home makes the thread continuously briefable and parallel-runnable alongside whatever product cycle is active. GOV-05 closes the first full route-out round-trip: GOV-04 routed F2/F3 to the telemetry-contract surface owner with a paper trail, and the GOV-05 fresh sweep is the verification gate that confirms the owner picked them up — proving the route-out discipline is a closed loop, not a fire-and-forget.
notes: GOV-05 closed 2026-05-14, same-day open-to-close, two loops clean. The sweep was clean — a light verification + codification sprint, not a backlog-execution one, and that is a valid sprint outcome (scoping from the sweep means accepting when the sweep is quiet). Primary finding (positive): GOV-04 L04 routed F2/F3 (telemetry-contract checker scheduling + failing contracts) out to the telemetry-contract surface owner with a paper trail; the GOV-05 sweep confirmed the owner picked them up — F2 scheduled (timer enabled + active), F3 closed (5/5 contracts passing). First full round-trip proof that the route-out discipline closes its loop at the next GOV sweep — codified to memory/route-out-verification-gate.md. GOV-05 carried nothing forward (GOV-02 lesson: carryover backlogs rot) — durable findings live in memory files, not in carryover. GOV-06 opens from a fresh sweep when next scheduled. Governance-thread workspace remains a checkout of dacumen.git; Casey Junior deployment 09c22323.
